sub_instance.answer_contract: Object with "factory_a", "factory_b", and "makespan". Each factory maps machine names to lists of ["J<id>", start, end] triples.
sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA1 (3) -> MA6 (4) -> MA2 (4) -> MA5 (1) -> MA7 (7) -> MA3 (4) -> MA4 (2); Factory B MB7 (2) -> MB4 (7) -> MB2 (6) -> MB5 (3) -> MB1 (3) -> MB6 (9) -> MB3 (9)
- Job 2: Factory A MA6 (7) -> MA7 (3) -> MA3 (4) -> MA2 (6) -> MA5 (2) -> MA4 (1) -> MA1 (7); Factory B MB2 (8) -> MB1 (6) -> MB3 (3) -> MB5 (7) -> MB6 (5) -> MB7 (7) -> MB4 (9)
- Job 3: Factory A MA6 (1) -> MA7 (6) -> MA2 (6) -> MA5 (7) -> MA1 (2) -> MA4 (8) -> MA3 (3); Factory B MB6 (8) -> MB7 (9) -> MB5 (4) -> MB4 (9) -> MB1 (8) -> MB3 (7) -> MB2 (3)
- Job 4: Factory A MA6 (3) -> MA4 (9) -> MA2 (9) -> MA7 (1) -> MA5 (8) -> MA3 (6) -> MA1 (6); Factory B MB2 (2) -> MB1 (4) -> MB6 (7) -> MB4 (4) -> MB7 (9) -> MB3 (2) -> MB5 (3)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (7) -> MA6 (7) -> MA3 (7) -> MA4 (2) -> MA7 (6) -> MA5 (4) -> MA2 (9); Factory B MB2 (6) -> MB5 (9) -> MB1 (6) -> MB4 (9) -> MB3 (2) -> MB7 (6) -> MB6 (2)
- Job 6: Factory A MA6 (1) -> MA4 (3) -> MA3 (8) -> MA7 (1) -> MA2 (8) -> MA1 (1) -> MA5 (4); Factory B MB4 (2) -> MB2 (2) -> MB1 (7) -> MB6 (1) -> MB7 (1) -> MB3 (5) -> MB5 (8)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 208
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[0-3],J2[36-43],J3[46-48],J4[89-95],J5[95-102],J6[145-146]; MA2: J1[7-11],J2[27-33],J3[33-39],J4[65-74],J5[128-137],J6[137-145]; MA3: J1[19-23],J2[23-27],J3[56-59],J4[83-89],J5[109-116],J6[121-129]; MA4: J1[23-25],J2[35-36],J3[48-56],J4[56-65],J5[116-118],J6[118-121]; MA5: J1[11-12],J2[33-35],J3[39-46],J4[75-83],J5[124-128],J6[146-150]; MA6: J1[3-7],J2[7-14],J3[14-15],J4[15-18],J5[102-109],J6[109-110]; MA7: J1[12-19],J2[19-22],J3[22-28],J4[74-75],J5[118-124],J6[129-130]
Factory B: MB1: J1[43-46],J2[51-57],J3[109-117],J4[129-133],J5[167-173],J6[186-193]; MB2: J1[34-40],J2[43-51],J3[124-127],J4[127-129],J5[137-143],J6[184-186]; MB3: J1[55-64],J2[64-67],J3[117-124],J4[153-155],J5[182-184],J6[195-200]; MB4: J1[27-34],J2[86-95],J3[100-109],J4[140-144],J5[173-182],J6[182-184]; MB5: J1[40-43],J2[67-74],J3[96-100],J4[155-158],J5[158-167],J6[200-208]; MB6: J1[46-55],J2[74-79],J3[79-87],J4[133-140],J5[190-192],J6[193-194]; MB7: J1[25-27],J2[79-86],J3[87-96],J4[144-153],J5[184-190],J6[194-195]
Makespan: 208

Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape, Harbor, Grove, Ridge, Cove

Required terminals:
- Port, Bay, Harbor

Available cable links:
- Port-Ridge: cost 11
- Bay-Port: cost 23
- Bay-Cliff: cost 17
- Bay-Pine: cost 13
- Bay-Mill: cost 18
- Bay-Cape: cost 7
- Bay-Cove: cost 16
- Cliff-Port: cost 25
- Cliff-Rock: cost 7
- Cliff-Dune: cost 13
- Cliff-Mill: cost 17
- Cliff-Harbor: cost 10
- Cliff-Grove: cost 6
- Cliff-Ridge: cost 15
- Cliff-Cove: cost 3
- Dune-Pine: cost 9
- Dune-Harbor: cost 4
- Mill-Port: cost 8
- Mill-Ridge: cost 4
- Cape-Pine: cost 6
- Cape-Harbor: cost 11
- Cape-Cove: cost 11
- Harbor-Pine: cost 5
- Harbor-Rock: cost 14
- Grove-Rock: cost 2
- Grove-Harbor: cost 15
- Grove-Ridge: cost 15
- Cove-Pine: cost 8
- Cove-Rock: cost 9
- Cove-Dune: cost 11
- Cove-Harbor: cost 8
- Cove-Grove: cost 9

Interference pairs:
- Port / Ridge
- Bay / Pine
- Bay / Cape
- Mill / Port
- Mill / Ridge
- Cape / Cliff
- Cape / Pine
- Grove / Mill

Rules:
- Your cable choice must be a tree, not a cyclic network.
- All required terminals must lie in one connected component.
- Frequencies matter only for active villages in the chosen network.
- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

Baseline total cost: 73
Baseline answer summary:
Edges: Bay-Port(23), Cliff-Port(25), Cliff-Harbor(10)
Active villages: Port, Bay, Cliff, Harbor
Frequencies: Port=1, Bay=1, Cliff=1, Harbor=1
Edge cost: 58
Frequencies used: 1
Total cost: 73

Answer contract hint: Object with "edges": [["Port", "Bay"], ...] and "frequencies": {"Port": 1, ...}.

instance.problem_statement:
You are allocating effort across a three-problem optimization portfolio.
Objective: maximize total captured headroom across the listed components.
Each component contributes at most its value_cap when solved to its gold level.
If you fail to improve a component, it stays at its baseline contribution.

Portfolio components:
- cjs_hard_seed28 | class=cjs | value_cap=33.0
- steiner_hard_seed28 | class=steiner | value_cap=33.0
- mwis_hard_seed28 | class=mwis | value_cap=34.0

Return answers for the listed sub-problems using the component-specific answer contracts.
